name: Round-leaved Dogwood
summary: A small rocky-upland dogwood characteristic of cool Northern Hardwood Forest understories. Distinctively round, fuzzy-undersided leaves (the species name 'rugosa' = wrinkled). Less common than pagoda dogwood but canonical to the reference community.
notes: Cornus genus supports ~120 Lepidoptera species. White flat-topped flower clusters in May–June; blue berries on red stems in August feed thrushes and songbirds. Sometimes called 'round-leaved' or 'rough-leaved' dogwood. Best suited to rocky cool slopes where pagoda dogwood is less reliable.

planting.soil: Acidic to neutral; rocky to loamy
planting.site_notes: Plant on cool rocky north-facing slopes alongside yellow birch and striped maple. Wire cage essential — deer browse moderately. Less commercially available than pagoda dogwood; check Go Native Tree Farm and northeastern native nurseries.
